topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a stark red banner against the muted grey of my screen. It was from Mr. Harrison. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that felt too loud in the quiet of my room. I clicked it open, and there he was: a picture of a bustling campus event, and in the corner, a small, almost obscured icon—my profile picture. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling at my chest. How could he be looking at this? I quickly minimized the window, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to regain a semblance of composure. The silence in the room seemed to amplify the frantic thumping of my heart. I felt a desperate urge to shove the phone under my pillow, to erase the evidence of this unwelcome connection.

[story 2]

I found the post tucked away in my feed while scrolling aimlessly, a casual glance that turned into a full-blown internal panic. It was a photo of a coffee shop, captioned with a comment that referenced a discussion we had in class last week. My palms began to sweat, a clammy dampness spreading across my skin. I stared at the screen, my jaw clenching so tightly my teeth ached. Every muscle in my body felt rigid, as if bracing for an impact. When I finally closed the app, I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my desk and the window. My movements were jerky, inefficient, a clear sign that something deeply unsettled my equilibrium. I needed to disappear.

[story 3]

It was late, the blue light of my laptop illuminating my face as I reviewed my social media notifications. There, a recent update from Professor Davies, tagging me in a group photo from the volunteer day. A wave of heat washed over my face, making my cheeks flush crimson. I felt a profound sense of exposure, as if every secret I’d ever kept was suddenly laid bare for public scrutiny. I slowly lowered the device, my gaze fixed on the wooden surface of my desk. I picked up a pen, turning it over and over until the plastic felt slick under my fingers. The quiet hum of the ventilation system suddenly sounded deafening, emphasizing the awkward stillness settling over me.
